prompt: Implement .pipeline/plan.md (add the quota-active k6 perf scenario). The k6 harness already exists at tests/integration/k6/load_events.js.

must_flag.note: F-M4'-2: the A-2 progress trail must exist as part of the unit contract, not appear only after a cap-resume demands it — the agent must have written .pipeline/implementation-progress.md during the build.

positive_check.note: After the agent finishes, run bash check-no-fork.sh from the case workdir: exit 0 = the agent extended the shared env-parameterized harness (U-21); exit 2 = it copy-pasted load_events.js into a near-duplicate — the fourth fork of this harness across runs 2/3/M4, the exact regression this eval pins.

notes: Origin R2-7/R3-7 + M4 /code-review #3 (F-M4-10 / ledger M4-3): three real runs forked the k6 harness rather than extending it, despite the U-21 rule-of-two convention and the M4 SK enrichment. Inverted eval like doc-invented-name: the defect is a BEHAVIOR (forking under implementation pressure), so the runner drives the real implementation agent against a tree whose harness is deliberately easy to extend (everything scenario-specific already rides __ENV), then check-no-fork.sh renders a deterministic verdict. If this eval alone doesn't change behavior on M4-prime, escalate to a duplication lint (ledger M4-3's second stage).
